name: drawer
title: Drawer
description: An edge-anchored dialog built on Base UI: focus trap, Esc to close, backdrop dismiss, scroll lock, and focus return to the trigger. Composed parts (Drawer, DrawerTrigger, DrawerContent, DrawerTitle, DrawerDescription, DrawerClose, DrawerHeader, DrawerFooter) with a required title and a 44px Close button. The side prop (start | end | top | bottom) is logical: start/end map to the inline-start/inline-end edges and flip in RTL; top/bottom sit on the block axis. Slide-in is motion-safe (transform) with a reduced-motion fade, the backdrop respects prefers-reduced-transparency, and the panel scrolls internally at 400% zoom.
useWhen: A side panel of supplementary controls that belong to the current page — filters, facets, or a secondary form the user can apply and dismiss
Primary navigation on small screens (a menu that slides in from the inline-start edge)
Contextual detail or a quick edit panel that should sit beside the task without navigating away
avoidWhen: A centered decision the user must resolve — confirmations, destructive actions, short blocking forms (use Dialog)
Inline supporting content that can expand in place within the page flow (use Collapsible, or Accordion for several sections)
A persistent layout region that is always visible (that is app chrome or a sidebar, not a drawer)
Content important or long enough to deserve its own page — deep links, printing, and back-button behavior all break in an overlay

keyboard: Enter or Space on the trigger opens the drawer; focus moves to the first tabbable element inside
Tab and Shift+Tab cycle through the drawer's controls only (focus is trapped while open)
Enter or Space on DrawerClose or the icon Close button closes the drawer
On close, focus returns to the trigger that opened the drawer
role=dialog with aria-modal while modal; the drawer is named by its DrawerTitle via aria-labelledby (a DrawerTitle or explicit aria-label is required — a dev warning fires otherwise)
